purpose: Public-safe display projections for turning rollout and trajectory evidence into reusable frontstage visuals.

truth_contract.evidence_floor: public GitHub PR metadata and public Git history
truth_contract.recompute_rule: Regenerate projections from public event sources before treating them as current.

projection_model.description: A projection maps source evidence into metrics, actor/state stages, lanes, nodes, and edges. The frontstage renderer consumes this model without knowing whether the source was a PR batch, agent rollout, issue-fix loop, benchmark route, or long-running LoopX iteration.
projection_model.node_contract: Nodes are compact public-safe work units such as pull_request, event, todo, gate, validation, or artifact.
projection_model.edge_contract: Edges are compact public-safe relationships such as review, followup, handoff, revert, validation, dependency, or successor.

projections.title: Overnight PR batch with reviewable control

scene.title: Review mesh over a 30-PR public batch
scene.explanation: A generic rollout projection turns public PR metadata into a visible state graph: PR-sized slices move through runtime, product, benchmark, docs, and review lanes, then converge through validation and merge gates.
scene.why_current: This is the first concrete projection instance after the PR #674 trajectory panel: it exercises the generic renderer with a larger public PR/review batch.

source_contract.claim_boundary: Display projection only. Public GitHub PR metadata and public repository history are the evidence floor; raw logs, private chats, local state, and private planning material are excluded.
source_contract.next_projection_hint: LoopX overall iteration should reuse this projection model instead of adding another bespoke panel.

timeline.title: Overnight wall-clock timeline
timeline.description: The timeline is a wall-clock public-safe work-unit axis. This instance positions PR nodes by public GitHub createdAt and shows completedAt/duration on hover; other rollout projections can provide issue, todo, run, or trace timestamps.
timeline.time_basis: node.started_at positions each work unit; node.completed_at and duration_label explain review/merge latency

mapping_layers.label: Source intake
mapping_layers.description: Public PR metadata is reduced into compact work-unit facts without importing raw chats, local state, or private traces.

mapping_layers.label: State projection
mapping_layers.description: Each work unit is mapped onto claim, plan, edit, validate, review, and resume stages so a single agent's progress is visible.

mapping_layers.label: Lane routing
mapping_layers.description: The same nodes are grouped into runtime, product, benchmark, docs, and open-review lanes so multi-agent flow is legible.

mapping_layers.label: Edge reasoning
mapping_layers.description: Review, revert, validation, CLI, automation, and runtime followups form a mesh that explains why the next slice exists.

mapping_layers.label: Operator readout
mapping_layers.description: The renderer highlights merged throughput, open review, follow-up chains, and the next reusable projection target.

flow_signals.label: Throughput
flow_signals.description: Nearly the whole public batch landed during the visible wall-clock window.

flow_signals.label: Review resolution
flow_signals.description: The projection keeps the closed/non-merged branch visible as a review outcome instead of hiding it.

flow_signals.label: Cross-lane edges
flow_signals.description: Relationships cross product, runtime, docs, and benchmark lanes.

flow_signals.label: Reusable model
flow_signals.description: The same renderer can later map issue-fix, content ops, benchmark, or LoopX iteration rollouts.

relationship_summaries.label: Monitor/runtime chain
relationship_summaries.description: Watch routing moves into due selection and poll writeback.

relationship_summaries.label: Validation chain
relationship_summaries.description: Runtime API work is followed by smoke coverage.

relationship_summaries.label: Showcase chain
relationship_summaries.description: Public cases become pages and then a canonical showcase path.

relationship_summaries.label: Review correction
relationship_summaries.description: A public surface review creates a fast revert slice.

relationship_summaries.label: Command loop
relationship_summaries.description: Newcomer command path feeds an explicit PR review command.

attention_hotspots.label: Runtime chain resolved
attention_hotspots.description: Monitor scheduler smoke and CLI metadata moved through review and merge; the chain remains visible as a completed follow-up path.

attention_hotspots.label: Adapter review resolved
attention_hotspots.description: The memory adapter contract also merged later in the same wall-clock window, showing how a long review branch rejoins the batch.

attention_hotspots.label: Next projection target
attention_hotspots.description: After this PR batch projection is accepted, LoopX overall iteration can reuse the same renderer.

rollout_sequence.title: Requirement rollout spine
rollout_sequence.description: One demand unlocks the next: each requirement becomes a reviewable slice, validates against the public boundary, and leaves the next requirement visible.

units.requirement: Show frontstage trajectory as a reusable projection
Turn reusable cases into public showcase pages
units.outcome: Case set, showcase pages, and canonical path landed.

units.requirement: Publish biweekly update notes without relying on memory
units.outcome: Archive plus draft PR automation are now visible.

units.requirement: Monitor tasks become due work instead of hidden polling
units.outcome: Watch routing, due selection, and writeback landed; smoke and CLI metadata remain open.

units.requirement: Keep goal-start benchmark routing attributed while product work evolves
units.triggered_by: solver lifecycle needs a stable bridge
units.outcome: Goal-start bridge, closeout attribution, watchdog, and ledger updates landed.

units.requirement: Let users drive review and newcomer commands explicitly
units.triggered_by: slash commands should be product entry points
units.outcome: Newcomer command path feeds an explicit PR review command.

units.requirement: Route explicit peer review handoffs by task policy instead of profile or prose
units.triggered_by: multi-agent rollout needs a clean next receiver
units.outcome: PR #775 keeps the open handoff route visible for review.

stages.label: Claim
stages.description: Pick one scoped todo and reserve the lane.

stages.label: Plan
stages.description: Split the target into ordered public-safe slices.

stages.label: Edit
stages.description: Apply a narrow patch against one product or runtime surface.

stages.label: Validate
stages.description: Run focused smokes and boundary checks before writeback.

stages.label: Review
stages.description: Attach review packet, handoff, or self-merge decision.

stages.label: Resume
stages.description: Use the next public todo or monitor due signal for the following loop.

lanes.label: Kernel runtime
lanes.summary: Status, quota, monitor, handoff, and todo projection contracts.

lanes.label: Product frontstage
lanes.summary: Public showcase pages, trajectory panels, command onboarding, and update notes.

lanes.label: Benchmark interface
lanes.summary: Goal-start bridge, runner attribution, reducer preservation, and latest ledger updates.

lanes.label: Docs and showcase
lanes.summary: Public-safe case set, local agent launch protocol, release readiness, and adoption loop.

lanes.label: Open review lane
lanes.summary: Adapter and branch work that stays visible as a review gate.

nodes.title: Fix task-scoped peer review handoff routing
Bootstrap SkillsBench goal-start bridge before task

Add agent lane frontier hint contract
Accept Harbor reducer wrapper in run ledger upsert

must_render: node time and duration hover details

planned_projections.reason: Use the same projection model after the overnight PR batch renderer is validated.
